subject: Sports
title: Hydration: The 2% Rule
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Hydration: The 2% Rule.

hook.text: Losing just 2% of your body weight in sweat can drop athletic performance by up to 25%.

buildup.text: Dehydration thickens blood, raises heart rate, and impairs the body's ability to cool itself.

discovery.text: Athletes can lose 1-2 liters of sweat per hour during intense exercise. Electrolytes go with it.

twist.text: Over-hydrating is dangerous too. Hyponatremia—too much water, too little sodium—can be fatal in extreme cases.

climax.text: Modern sports drinks balance water, sodium, and carbs to replace exactly what the body loses during competition.

punchline.text: Too little water kills performance. Too much can be worse.

quiz.question: What happens when an athlete loses 2% of body weight through sweat?
options: Performance can drop by up to 25%
